title: ageratina dorrii
intro: Ageratina dorrii, also known as Mormon tea or White Sage, is a plant species native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico, belonging to the mint family Lamiaceae. Traditionally, Ageratina dorrii has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiviral properties, derived from its high content of flavonoids and saponins. The plant has been associated with various health benefits, such as reducing fever, relieving respiratory issues, and supporting digestive health, although more research is needed to confirm its effectiveness. Ageratina dorrii can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture for medicinal purposes, and is also sometimes used in aromatherapy due to its calming and decongestant properties.
